content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Debugging API Integrations with AI
content.seo_description: Master API integration debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. A professional, intermediate-level workflow to ship faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing logs. Solve API integration failures by abstracting your request cycle into Claude Code, then using Antigravity to simulate edge-case payloads. By decoupling the network layer from your logic, you isolate errors instantly, ensuring robust, production-ready code that keeps your client projects moving at breakneck speed.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a 403 Forbidden or a silent 500 error, and the clock is ticking on your client’s deadline. The code looks fine, the documentation is vague, and the integration is failing in ways that don't make sense. This is the 'Integration Purgatory' that kills freelance momentum and ruins professional reputations.

But what if you could treat your API interactions like a modular, predictable system? By leveraging modern AI-native tools, you can move from 'guess-and-check' debugging to a surgical, evidence-based workflow. This guide isn't about reading docs—it's about building a repeatable, high-speed pipeline that turns complex API issues into solved tickets in minutes.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before debugging, codify the expected schema to prevent type mismatches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Zod schema based on the API response documentation provided in the context. Ensure all fields are typed correctly and handle nullability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate Zod schema for the API endpoint at ./src/api/client.ts'

steps.title: Isolate the Request Layer
steps.description: Abstract your API client to make it testable and observable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this fetch call into a modular service class with built-in logging and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor ./lib/api.ts to use a robust service pattern with error interceptors'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Antigravity to inject failure states into your local environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that simulates 401, 404, and 500 API responses to verify my error handling logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create integration tests in ./tests/api.test.ts simulating failure states'

steps.title: Automate Log Analysis
steps.description: Feed your raw logs into Claude Code to identify patterns in your failed requests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these logs for patterns. Are there specific headers or payloads causing the failure?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze ./logs/api.log and identify recurring error patterns'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Retries
steps.description: Wrap your integration in a jittered exponential backoff strategy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a retry mechanism with exponential backoff and jitter to the existing API service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add retry logic to ./lib/api.ts using an exponential backoff strategy'

pro_tips: Always log the raw request/response headers before hashing; most auth issues are hidden in plain sight.
Use mock servers like Prism to validate your client implementation before hitting the actual production API.
Tag your API logs with a unique Correlation ID to trace requests across microservices.
Never hardcode API keys; use a local .env.local file and validate it at runtime with a startup script.
If an API fails intermittently, check your rate limits—Claude Code can write a tracker to alert you before you hit the threshold.
content.next_steps: Once your integration is stable, document the final API schema in your client's Notion or README to ensure long-term maintainability.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start shipping features that scale. With the right AI-powered workflow, you turn hours of debugging into minutes of execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
